win: sat down and did 60min on tracker app
lesson: need to set intention before deep work or get lost in small random tasks
change: write ONE goal on sticky before starting each deep work block

win: Did OMAD and noticed energy feedback in real time
lesson: Large single meal at ~3 PM was followed by a strong sleepy crash by ~5:30 PM; could be post-meal dip and/or weed recovery fatigue.
change: Tomorrow run a simple test: smaller meal load or split intake and add a 10-15 min walk post-meal; log energy at +1h/+2h/+3h.

win: Day 2 clean - stayed honest about feelings instead of numbing
lesson: Feeling very down and lonely. Moved from Calgary 5 years ago and haven't rebuilt a friend circle in Houston. Catching myself focusing on what I don't have instead of what I do. Unsure if withdrawal sadness or real sadness - probably both. Last time meditation helped pull out of this.
change: Restart meditation. Life is what you make it - there is a lot to be thankful for. Focus on gratitude not absence.

win: Recognized the binge/crash pattern - it applies to fitness too not just substances
lesson: Addictive personality goes 100% at everything including fitness → unsustainable intensity → crash → revert to binge mode. The same all-or-nothing wiring that drives substance abuse drives overtraining.
change: Monitor intensity in fitness - sustainable > maximal. When noticing 100% mode kicking in flag it as the same addiction pattern.

win: Recognized compensation pattern - using weed discipline as excuse to slip on eating
lesson: Dopamine-seeking drives food impulse — heading to Chick-fil-A despite knowing its unhealthy and not needing it. Feeling of cant control it signals automatic behavior not conscious choice.
change: Before eating out pause and ask: what am I feeling? Hunger or dopamine? Start identifying the trigger before the meal not after.

win: answered 7 clarity questions honestly - confronted people-pleasing pattern
lesson: attraction and respect are there but enjoyment and intrinsic choice are uncertain - scarcity fear is driving part of the decision
change: pause engagement timeline - start therapy for ROCD assessment - journal daily for 90 days before deciding

win: showed up to reflect even with scattered mind
lesson: morning fog and catastrophizing are likely withdrawal-related cognitive distortion — fragmented attention and negativity bias are hallmarks of dopamine recalibration
change: before spiraling into too many things do a 5-min brain dump to externalize noise then pick ONE thing aligned to vision

win: identified AI fluency as highest-leverage weekly focus
lesson: fluency is a multiplier — improving how I use AI directly improves every output including this app. Vision page needs a focus stack (day/week/month/year) but not ready to build yet — log the intent first
change: this week: prioritize AI fluency practice over new features. Park the vision page redesign as a future build.

win: Recognized overconsumption pattern and its impact on mental calm
lesson: Too much optional consumption — dopamine overload counteracts calm mind goal. Feeling overwhelmed and down possibly from overstimulation.
change: Document all consumption channels and audit which ones serve calm mind vs. drain it
